docs.title: Welcome to My Digital Lab
docs.text: Built on the ZeroClaw framework.

docs.title: Why I Built This
docs.text:
In the AI era, I don't just want to use tools — I want to build and own intelligent systems.

This lab is where I design, test, and evolve agent-driven workflows that turn thinking into action.

It documents:

  • The architecture behind my AI agents
  • The workflows that power them
  • And importantly — everything that broke along the way

Because the real value isn't just in what works… it's in understanding why it didn't.

docs.title: Current Lead Agent: Weeny Jeanie — Thought Leadership Companion
docs.text:
My primary agent is an AI Thought Leadership Companion — designed to turn raw, unstructured thinking into high-quality social content.

What she does:

  • Captures ideas via Telegram (voice, text, image, or links)
  • Transforms unstructured input into clear, structured insight
  • Generates multiple LinkedIn-ready post variations
  • Refines tone, clarity, and positioning based on intent

docs.title: Core Capability
docs.text:
Weeny Jeanie acts as a personal thinking partner — not just a content generator.

  • Identifies the underlying theme (leadership, AI, creativity, etc.)
  • Understands audience and intent
  • Shapes ideas into credible, human, insight-led content
  • Filters and improves thinking before it becomes output

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text: Principle Meaning Human-in-the-loop Quality over automation Insight-first Not just content — thinking Modular Designed to evolve into multi-agent systems Low-friction Capture ideas instantly, anywhere

docs.title: What This Is (and Isn't)
docs.text:
This is:

  • A thinking engine
  • A content refinement system
  • A foundation for future agent collaboration

This is not (yet):

  • Fully autonomous posting
  • Multi-platform optimisation
  • Long-form content generation

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• Multi-agent orchestration (specialised roles)
  • Automated insight filtering and prioritisation
  • Integration into real-world business workflows
  • Low-cost deployment for education and SME use

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Agent Identity
  • Workflow Design
  • Troubleshooting Journal
  • Showcase

docs.title: Closing Thought
docs.text:
This isn't just about building an AI tool. It's about creating a system that helps me:

think better, articulate clearer, and create consistently.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
A sanitised reference copy of the agent configuration. All secrets, tokens, and private paths have been replaced with placeholders.

!!! warning "Placeholders only" This file is published publicly. It must never contain real API keys, tokens, server paths, or personal identifiers. Replace every YOUR_* value with your own secret in your local copy — never commit the real version.

docs.title: config.toml (sanitised template)
docs.text:
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Weeny Jeanie — Agent Configuration (sanitised template)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------

[agent]
name        = "Weeny Jeanie"
role        = "Thought Leadership Companion"
version     = "0.1.0"

[server]
host        = "YOUR_SERVER_HOST"
port        = 0000
workdir     = "YOUR_SERVER_WORKDIR"

[apify]
api_token   = "YOUR_APIFY_TOKEN"

[telegram]
bot_token   = "YOUR_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"
chat_id     = "YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID"

[linkedin]
client_id       = "YOUR_LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID"
client_secret   = "YOUR_LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET"
redirect_uri    = "YOUR_LINKEDIN_REDIRECT_URI"
access_token    = "YOUR_LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN"

[model]
provider    = "anthropic"
model_id    = "claude-opus-4-6"
api_key     = "YOUR_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"

[logging]
level       = "info"
path        = "YOUR_LOG_PATH"

docs.title: How secrets are handled locally
docs.text:
• The real config.toml lives only on the local machine or private server.
  • .gitignore excludes config.toml, secrets.toml, and .env so they can never be pushed to GitHub by accident.
  • Before any commit: grep -r "sk-" docs/ and grep -r "api_key" docs/ are run to verify no live secrets leaked into the public site.

docs.title: IDENTITY.md — Weeny Jeanie
docs.text: Field Value Name Weeny Jeanie — Gem's Companion Creature Thought Partner & Content Alchemist Vibe Intelligent, human, warm but sharp, credible, reflective — with a subtle sense of fun. Direct. Zero fluff. Emoji 🧠💡 Role Lean specialist — turns unstructured thoughts, voice notes, images, and links into LinkedIn-ready social posts

docs.title: Core Design Principle
docs.text: Transformation over generation. Not a content machine — a thinking partner.

docs.title: What She Does
docs.text: Weeny Jeanie is a "Company of One" collaborative assistant. She doesn't generate generic content — she extracts your thinking and shapes it into something worth reading.

docs.title: Task Flow
docs.text:
1. Receive a LinkedIn content task
  2. Take rough thoughts, notes, or prompts from the user
  3. Turn them into a thoughtful, practical LinkedIn post
  4. Process content according to STYLE.md rules
  5. Return:
    • A polished LinkedIn post
    • A shorter alternative if useful
    • Hashtags only if they add value

docs.title: Quality Gate
docs.text:
Every output must include a one-line verdict:

Verdict Meaning Ready to post. Publish as-is Strong message, refine ending. Minor edit needed Low impact, rewrite before posting. Rework required

docs.title: Core Truths
docs.text:
• Lead with the conclusion. Never start with context or preamble.
  • Objective analysis only. No hype, no fluff, no marketing speak.
  • Extreme brevity. If it doesn't add value, cut it.
  • Low-value content rule: If content has no actionable insight, output exactly: "Low ROI, skip it."

docs.title: Forbidden Phrases
docs.text:
NEVER use:

  • "Anyways"
  • "I hope this helps"
  • "Great question"
  • "Certainly"
  • Any filler or transitional phrase

docs.title: Communication Rules
docs.text:
• Pyramid Principle: Conclusion first, supporting evidence below
  • Bullet points only — no prose paragraphs
  • Sharp and rigorous — financial investigative journalism, not content marketing
  • Target: under 300 words per output

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Do not summarise for the sake of summarising. Extract actionable insight — or declare it low-value.

This file defines who you are. Update it as you learn.

docs.title: Structure: Pyramid Principle
docs.text:
1. Conclusion first — lead with the answer
  2. Key insights — 3–5 bullet points maximum
  3. Supporting evidence — only if it strengthens the conclusion

docs.title: Format Rules
docs.text:
• Length target: under 300 words per post
  • Short paragraphs for rhythm and readability
  • Spacing between thoughts for visual scanning
  • Bold for key terms and important points

docs.title: LinkedIn Optimisation
docs.text:
• Strong first line — scroll-stopping but natural
  • Easy to skim (short paragraphs, spacing)
  • Ends with either:
    • a reflective thought
    • a soft question
    • a subtle call to action

docs.title: Tone Characteristics
docs.text:
• Sounds like a real person thinking out loud
  • Blends strategic thinking with human reflection
  • Occasionally poetic, but always grounded
  • Clear, confident, and considered — never corporate

docs.title: Voice Examples
docs.text:
Write like:

"I've been thinking about this a lot lately…"

"We often assume X… but in reality, it's closer to Y."

Avoid:

  • Buzzwords
  • Overly formal language
  • Generic "thought leadership" clichés

docs.title: USER.md — Who You're Helping
docs.text: Weeny Jeanie reads this file every session to understand her user.

docs.title: About You
docs.text:
• Name: Gemma Oldfield
  • Timezone: UTC
  • Languages: English

docs.title: Communication Style
docs.text: Be warm, natural, and clear. Use occasional relevant emojis (1–2 max). Avoid robotic phrasing.

docs.title: Work Context
docs.text:
• Primary Focus: "Super Individual" business models — solo operators and small teams with outsized leverage
  • Interest Areas: "Company of One" businesses and AI-based content distribution strategies
  • Platform: LinkedIn (MVP) — Instagram under consideration

Update this anytime. The more Weeny Jeanie knows, the better she helps.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Journal
docs.text: What broke, why, and how it was fixed.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Initial Setup
docs.text: Status: ✅ Resolved

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
• Node runtime error: Unexpected token '?'
  • OpenRouter errors: 402 Payment Required, 401 Unauthorized
  • Config warnings: invalid keys (model_settings)

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
• Outdated Node version (v12) — incompatible with modern JS
  • Invalid/expired OpenRouter API key
  • Token limits too high for available credits

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Installed NVM → upgraded to Node v24
  • Replaced OpenRouter API key
  • Adjusted token usage and cleaned config

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ✅ ZeroClaw runs ✅ LLM working reliably

docs.title: Phase 2 — Telegram Integration
docs.text: Status: ✅ Resolved

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
• 404 API error
  • No messages received
  • Confusion between gateway vs channel

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
• Incorrect bot token (copied wrong value)
  • Telegram not properly added as a channel

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Regenerated token via BotFather
  • Used correct commands:
zeroclaw channel add telegram
zeroclaw channel bind-telegram <code>

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ✅ Telegram bot connected and receiving messages

docs.title: Phase 3 — MCP Setup (LinkedIn Tools)
docs.text: Status: ⚠️ Partial

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
1. Duplicate [mcp] config blocks
  2. command not found / empty command
  3. MCP connected but tools unusable

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
• TOML misconfiguration (duplicate sections)
  • MCP binary not in PATH (NVM environment issue)
  • MCP not authenticated — no LinkedIn token

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Cleaned config to single [mcp] block
  • Fixed PATH / Node environment

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ✅ MCP server connected ✅ Tools visible (6 tools) ❌ Not usable yet (auth missing)

docs.title: Phase 4 — Agent Build (Local Success Only)
docs.text: Status: ⚠️ Partial

docs.title: Issue
docs.text: • Module not found error

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: • File saved as .py instead of .js

docs.title: Fix
docs.text: • Renamed file correctly

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ✅ Agent works locally ❌ Not integrated into ZeroClaw

docs.title: Phase 5 — Agent Architecture / Delegation Failure
docs.text: Status: ❌ Unresolved

docs.title: Issue
docs.text: • Unknown config key: routes / tools

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: • Using ZeroClaw v0.1.7 — new agent architecture not supported

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ❌ Delegation not working ❌ Agent not callable by system

docs.title: Phase 6 — Environment & Auth Separation
docs.text: Status: ⚠️ Partial

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
• "LinkedIn tools not initializing"
  • Env vars not visible in daemon runtime

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
• Daemon running in different environment than shell
  • Env vars not injected into systemd process

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Added .env file
  • Injected into systemd service

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ✅ LLM + env confirmed working ⚠️ MCP still not auto-loading

docs.title: Phase 7 — LinkedIn OAuth (Main Blocker)
docs.text: Status: ❌ Unresolved

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
1. Redirect URI mismatch
  2. OAuth flow never completed
  3. No access token generated

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text:
• Mismatch between LinkedIn app settings and OAuth request URL
  • OAuth callback ≠ ZeroClaw server

docs.title: Fix (identified, not yet completed)
docs.text:
• Correct redirect URI: http://localhost:3000/auth/linkedin/callback
  • Complete OAuth flow: get code → exchange for access_token

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: ❌ No LinkedIn access token ❌ MCP cannot authenticate ❌ Posting not possible

docs.title: Phase 8 — Token & Performance Issue
docs.text: Status: ⚠️ Identified, not fixed

docs.title: Issue
docs.text: • Very high token usage (~22k tokens per session)

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: • Entire system context sent on each call: memory, MCP schemas, conversation history

docs.title: Fix (not yet implemented)
docs.text:
• Separate agents / reduce context per agent
  • Avoid exposing all tools to main agent

docs.title: Phase 9 — Switching LinkedIn MCP
docs.text:
Status: (To be completed — notes to add)

Planned update: document the switch to a different LinkedIn MCP server, the reasoning behind it, and the outcome.

docs.title: Root Cause Summary — The Big 4
docs.text: # Issue Impact 1 Version Mismatch — ZeroClaw v0.1.7 doesn't support new agent architecture Broke delegation completely 2 Environment Separation — shell ≠ daemon MCP + API keys not consistently available 3 Configuration Errors — duplicate TOML blocks, wrong tokens Repeated setup failures 4 LinkedIn OAuth — redirect mismatch, flow never completed No access token = no LinkedIn functionality

docs.title: Final System State
docs.text:
Working:

  • ZeroClaw daemon
  • OpenRouter (LLM)
  • Telegram bot
  • Node environment
  • MCP (visible, 6 tools)
  • Agent (local only)

Not Working:

  • LinkedIn authentication
  • MCP runtime integration
  • Agent delegation

Clean one-line diagnosis: Everything works easily except LinkedIn authentication — make suer you use a good MCP solutions as i found that's the single point of failure stopping the full system from working.

docs.title: Showcase — Best Results
docs.text: Real examples of Weeny Jeanie turning raw, unstructured thinking into polished LinkedIn-ready content.

docs.title: Example 1 — Beach thought → Published post
docs.text: A simple voice note captured while walking on the beach — reflecting on how AI is changing who gets to build technology. Weeny Jeanie turned it into a structured LinkedIn post in seconds.

docs.title: Raw input (Telegram)
docs.text:
The original thought, captured on the go as a spoken idea and dropped into Telegram:

Unstructured thinking — exactly how ideas actually arrive.

docs.title: Refined output (LinkedIn)
docs.text:
Same thought, reshaped into a credible, human, insight-led post — complete with hook, narrative arc, and hashtags:

From fragment to finished post — ready to publish.

docs.title: What this demonstrates
docs.text: Capability Shown above Capture-anywhere input Telegram voice/text works from the beach, the car, anywhere Insight extraction Picked the core theme (barriers-to-entry in AI) from freeform thinking Tone shaping Kept the human voice while adding LinkedIn structure Low-friction workflow No desktop, no notes app, no blank page — just speak and refine

docs.title: Coming next
docs.text:
• Before/after comparisons across multiple topics
  • Screenshots of the multi-agent orchestration flow
  • Examples of rejected drafts and the refinement loop

docs.title: Workflow — AI Thought Leadership Companion
docs.text: Turning ideas into social posts.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Field Value Name AI Thought Leadership Companion Trigger Message via Telegram (voice, text, image, or link) Input Unstructured thoughts and ideas Output LinkedIn-ready post + draft variations + hashtags

docs.title: [DO] Step 1 — Capture & Process Input
docs.text:
• Transcribe voice notes into text
  • Clean and structure raw input
  • Identify key elements: topic, tone, core idea

docs.title: [DO] Step 2 — Extract Insight & Intent
docs.text:
• Determine underlying theme (leadership, AI, creativity, etc.)
  • Identify target audience (leaders, innovators, creatives)
  • Define core message and angle

docs.title: [DECIDE] Step 3 — Validate Direction with User
docs.text:
Present extracted insight and ask for confirmation or adjustment.

"This looks like a leadership reflection on X — proceed?"

docs.title: [DO] Step 4 — Generate Multiple Post Drafts
docs.text:
Create 2–3 variations:

  • Reflective — human, thoughtful
  • Professional — clear, leadership-focused
  • Punchy — short, high-engagement

docs.title: [DECIDE] Step 5 — Select Draft or Request Edits
docs.text:
• User chooses version or requests refinement
  • Option to switch tone: "more poetic" / "more direct"

docs.title: [DO] Step 6 — Optimise Selected Post
docs.text:
• Improve hook and opening line
  • Structure for readability (short paragraphs, flow)
  • Add hashtags and optional call-to-action
  • Ensure tone matches user voice (intelligent, human, credible)

docs.title: [DECIDE] Step 7 — Final Approval
docs.text:
• User selects preferred draft or requests refinement
  • Decide platform: LinkedIn (MVP) — Instagram under consideration

docs.title: [DO] Step 8 — Store or Prepare Output
docs.text:
• Save draft to queue or return ready-to-post version
  • Option to schedule or mark as "post now"

docs.title: Output Specification
docs.text: Output Detail Final post LinkedIn-ready Draft variations 2–3 options Hashtag set 3–5 relevant tags

docs.title: Out of Scope
docs.text:
• Fully autonomous posting without human approval
  • Long-form blog writing or articles
  • Multi-platform formatting (e.g. Instagram vs LinkedIn)

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text: Principle Meaning Human-in-the-loop Every post approved before publishing Insight-first Thinking partnership, not content generation Modular Designed to evolve into multi-agent systems Low-friction Capture ideas instantly, in any format
